free_text: Intrinsic states in odd-A rhenium isotopes
Reaction products were detected in Kodak-type
NTB nuclear emulsions in the focal surface of an Enge
broad-range spectrograph.
Targets were prepared by evaporating tungsten metal
in an electron-gun evaporator. The thickness of the
target material was about 50 mu-g/cm2, and was
deposited on carbon foil 20 mu-g/cm2 in thickness.
Transfer l-values were obtained from cross-
section ratios of the two stripping reactions and
spectroscopic factors were extracted using DWBA
calculations.
OG
For the (He3,d) reaction the emulsions were covered by
aluminum absorber 0.9 mm in thickness.
No information on source of uncertainties

covered by 0.l-mm aluminum foil to absorb
singly-charged, inelastically scattered alpha
particles.
